LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JOE QUINN BRETZ AND RANDOLPH BRETZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS BRETT TODOROFF AND MARK FINLEY'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATIONS DEFENDANTS SEEK TO STRIKE FORM A BASIS FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISSOLUTION OF THE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, TO WHICH DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT DEMURRED, AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, WHICH THE COURT PREVIOUSLY RULED IS SUFFICIENTLY PLED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT "ADMITTED" THAT THE SOLE CONTRACT AT ISSUE IN THIS ACTION IS THE SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RANDOLPH BRETZ'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. OFF CALENDAR. "BEFORE FILING A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS PURSUANT TO THIS CHAPTER, THE MOVING PARTY SHALL MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE'" (CCP 439 (EMPHASIS ADDED).) Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiffs For Order Imposing Sanctions Of $5,00.00 Against Attorney David P. Nemecek, Jr., Of The Fortress Law Firm, Inc., For Motion For Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs BRETT TODOROFF and MARK FINLEY's Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions Against Attorney David P. Nemecek, Jr., Of The Fortress Law Firm, Inc., For Motion For Judgment

Plaintiffs Brett Todoroff and Mark Finley's motion for sanctions against attorney David P. Nemecek, Jr. is denied without prejudice to their renewing the motion before the assigned trial judge. Plaintiffs seek sanctions against Defendants' counsel for filing what they contend was a repetitive and duplicative motion for judgment on the pleadings. On May 20, 2019, this Court took that matter off calendar due to the parties' failure to meet and confer in person or by telephone regarding the basis for the motion as required by Code of Civil Procedure Section 439(a). Defendants' counsel apparently has refiled the motion on behalf of Defendant Randolph Bretz to be heard at the outset of trial, which is scheduled for Monday, June 17, 2019. Plaintiffs may renew their request for sanctions before the trial court.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar May-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David P. Nemecek Jr, Esq., Fortress Law Firm, telephoen 415-659-1946, , appearing for Defendant Joe Quinn Bretz;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RANDOLPH BRETZ'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

OFF CALENDAR. "Before filing a motion for judgment on the pleadings pursuant to this chapter, the moving party shall meet and confer in person or by telephone'" (CCP 439 (emphasis added).) The declaration of David Nemecek fails to show that the parties met and conferred in compliance with the statute. The court will not entertain defendants' alternative common law motion for judgment on the pleadings as a means to circumvent the meet and confer requirement.

Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOE BRETZ, RANDOLPH BRETZ's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint.

Defendants Joe Quinn Bretz and Randolph Bretz's motion to strike portions of Plaintiffs Brett Todoroff and Mark Finley's first amended complaint is denied. The allegations Defendants seek to strike form a basis for the first cause of action for dissolution of the limited liability company, to which Defendants have not demurred, and the second cause of action for breach of contract, which the Court previously ruled is sufficiently pled. Plaintiffs have not "admitted" that the sole contract at issue in this action is the Subscription Agreement.

Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

MINI MINUTES:

Jason A. Pollack, Esq. of Law Offices of Jason Pollack, 310.824.8733, appearing for the Plaintiff;
David P. Nemecek, Jr., Esq. of Fortress Law Firm, 415.659.1946, appearing for the Defendants.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants Joe Quinn Bretz And Randolph Bretz For Relief From Judgment is called. ARGUMENT held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Defendants Joe Bretz and Randolph Bretz's motion for relief from judgment under CCP 473(b) is GRANTED. California law favors trials on the merits, and strong public policy favors granting the relief provided by CCP 473(b) when parties move for relief within six months of the judgment. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 981-82.). Order signed. (see order for full details)
